div.p: and keep it all in silence, not even mention them again;1 then you will be established in power, virtue,2 and holiness, and the wrath of God will be turned away.3

I have one request to make of the President and members of the society, that you search yourselves—the tongue is an unruly member5—hold your tongues about things of no moment—a little tale will set the world on fire.6 At this time, the truth on the guilty should not be told openly,7 strange as this may seem, yet this is a policy. We must use precaution in bringing sinners to justice, lest in exposing these heinous sins we draw the indignation of a Gentile world upon us (and, to their imagination, justly too). It is necessary to hold an influence in the world, and thus spare ourselves an extermination; and also accomplish our end in spreading the Gospel, or holiness, in the earth. If we were brought to desolation, the disobedient would find no help. There are some who are obedient, yet men cannot steady the ark8—my arm cannot do it—God must steady it. To the iniquitous show yourselves merciful.

I am advised by some of the heads of the Church to tell the Relief Society to be virtuous, but to save the Church from desolation and the sword;9 beware, be still, be prudent,10 repent, reform, but do it in a way not to destroy all around you.11 I do not want to cloak iniquity—all things contrary to the will of God,12 should be cast from us, but don’t do more hurt than good, with your tongues—be pure in heart.13 Jesus designs to save the people out of their sins.14 Said Jesus, “Ye shall do the work, which ye see me do.”15 These are the grand key-words16 for the society to act upon. If I were not in your midst to aid and counsel you, the devil would overcome you.17 I want the innocent to go free—rather spare ten iniquitous among you, than condemn one innocent one.18 “Fret not thyself because of evildoers.”19 God will see to it. (May 26, 1842.) DHC 5:19–21.
div.div: MINUTES OF MEETING OF THE FEMALE RELIEF SOCIETY,
div.p: President Joseph Smith opened the meeting by prayer, and then addressed the congregation on the design of the insti-
